target.title: feat(exec): add normalized auto mode
target.body:
## Summary

Adds normalized `tools.exec.mode` support for host exec policy and maps `tools.exec.mode: "auto"` to Guardian-reviewed Codex app-server execution.

The native `auto` path uses deterministic allowlist/safe-bin matches directly. On approval misses, it asks the model-backed exec reviewer for a one-shot allow decision. Anything else routes to human approval.

## Scope Boundary

Included:

- `tools.exec.mode` values: `deny`, `allowlist`, `ask`, `auto`, `full`.
- Native gateway and node exec auto-review on approval misses.
- Codex app-server mapping from OpenClaw `auto` to Guardian/`auto_review` approvals.
- Plugin SDK subpath for exec approval policy helpers so the Codex plugin does not import core internals.
- Docs/schema/baseline updates for the new config surface.

Not included:

- No session-persisted `execMode`.
- No `execOverrides.mode`.
- No slash `/exec mode=...` directive surface.
- No auto-reply/session patch persistence changes.
- No unrelated bug-fix backlog items.

## Behavior Notes

- If native auto-review returns `allow-once`, OpenClaw records and resolves a suppressed one-shot approval.
- If native auto-review returns `ask`, malformed output, or anything unsupported, OpenClaw routes to human approval.
- If auto-review asks for human approval, `askFallback=full` does not execute the command.
- Bound native Codex conversation binding rejects touched exec policies that require interactive human approvals, because that path cannot route the approval back to a human.
- Direct node `system.run` auto-review requires the prepared `systemRunPlan` before approved scoped execution.
- Security-audit suppression edits stay on the human approval path in auto mode.
- Sandboxed `exec` still works when global `tools.exec.mode: "auto"` is configured and `tools.exec.host` stays on the default `auto` route.
- Legacy `security=full, ask=on-miss` stays full execution behavior; only `full/always` prompts.

## Verification

```text

```

Result: 24 files passed, 690 tests passed.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.buildembeddedsandboxinfo.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/agents/exec-defaults.test.ts
```

Result: 6 files passed, 194 tests passed.

```text
git diff --check
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
pnpm docs:list
codex review --base origin/main
codex review --base origin/main
codex review --base origin/main
```

Result: whitespace checks passed, docs list passed, and three consecutive Codex reviews reported no actionable correctness issues on the final tree.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Native OpenClaw `tools.exec.mode: "auto"` and Codex app-server Guardian-reviewed approvals now share one normalized config surface.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the focused Vitest suites above.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused exec/Codex test command, the affected regression test command, both whitespace checks, `pnpm docs:list`, and three consecutive `codex review --base origin/main` passes.

Evidence after fix: Focused suites passed with 24 files and 690 tests; affected regression tests passed with 6 files and 194 tests; final PR diff is 66 files, +5388/-491.

Observed result after fix: Auto-review allow paths, auto-review-to-human paths, fallback-full blocking, sandbox exec with global auto mode, Codex app-server policy mapping, node `system.run` approval-plan enforcement, security-suppression human review, legacy `full/on-miss` reporting, and config/schema behavior are covered by passing tests.

What was not tested: Live Codex app-server Guardian end-to-end approval flow.

## Compatibility / Migration

Existing `tools.exec.security` and `tools.exec.ask` continue to work. New configs should prefer `tools.exec.mode` for the normalized policy. Explicit lower-scope legacy policy values still take precedence where they already apply.

target.title: perf(plugins): thread prepared manifestPlugins through runtime model-id normalize chain
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the prepared-manifest-metadata threading from #82814 into the runtime plugin-normalize branch so callers that already carry prepared `manifestPlugins` skip the residual `installs.json` slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. Closes the gap that #82814's measurements left visible (Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` was at 28 metadata scans per cold+warm sample post-#82814).

Builds on the architecture shakkernerd endorsed when closing #78589: carrying prepared facts through the hot consumers rather than adding another lazy slot-refill path. Does not touch `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` itself.

## Why

`#82814` (`fix: reuse plugin manifest metadata safely`, merged 2026-05-17) threaded prepared `manifestPlugins` through the static model-id normalize path (`normalizeStaticProviderModelId` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`) so consumers stop consulting the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` single-slot handoff in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. That eliminated most of the hot-path `installs.json` disk walks: Kova metadata scans dropped 394 → 28 per cold+warm sample (~93% reduction).

The remaining 28 scans come from the runtime plugin-normalize branch. `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts` already receives `options.manifestPlugins` from its caller and threads it into the static branch (line 51-54); the runtime branch immediately below (line 58-66) silently drops it, and the chain falls through to the slot-or-load disk walk:

\`\`\`
src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59
  → normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime(...)         (no plugins param)
    → normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin(params)     (no plugins param)
      → normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params) (params.plugins undefined)
        → loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies   (slot-or-load disk walk)
\`\`\`

`normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest` already honors `params.plugins` to short-circuit:

\`\`\`ts
function loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params) {
  if (params.plugins) {
    return collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins);  // prepared path
  }
  const { snapshot, cacheable } = resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies(params);  // slot-or-load
  // ...
}
\`\`\`

The intermediate functions just never accepted a `plugins` field. This PR adds it as an additive optional param and forwards `options?.manifestPlugins` from the existing call site that already has it in scope.

## How

Three surgical edits:

1. **`src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts`** — add optional `plugins?: readonly Pick<PluginManifestRecord, \"modelIdNormalization\">[]` to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime`'s params. The body just forwards `params` unchanged to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`, so no behavioral change beyond the type signature.

2. **`src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`** — add the same optional `plugins?` field to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`'s params. The existing `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params)` call already forwards everything, so `plugins` flows straight through to where it's honored.

3. **`src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59`** — at the runtime call site, thread `options?.manifestPlugins` into the runtime call. Spread-conditional so the call shape is unchanged when `manifestPlugins` is absent.

Net diff: +6 LOC across three source files (plus tests and changelog).

## Relationship to the previously closed PR #78589

`#78589` proposed a different fix at a different layer: refilling the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` slot after a cold load inside `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. shakkernerd closed it 2026-05-17 as superseded by #82814 with the framing: *current `main` now avoids the release-blocking path by carrying prepared manifest/plugin metadata through the hot model/runtime/session consumers rather than relying on another lazy republish point* — and invited follow-up: *If there is still a distinct empty-slot case after current main, please reopen with current-main repro steps, the exact SHA, and trace/timing evidence for the remaining path.*

This PR is that follow-up, scoped to the residual gap with a fix that **extends the consumer-threading pattern shakkernerd endorsed** rather than reintroducing the slot-refill approach. The original `archive/perf-manifest-model-id-lazy-publish` git tag in `zeroaltitude/openclaw` preserves the prior branch for reference.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Callers that pass prepared `manifestPlugins` to `normalizeModelRef` (via `ModelManifestNormalizationContext`) had that metadata silently dropped when the runtime plugin-normalize branch was taken, causing the chain to fall through to the slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` even though prepared metadata was available in the caller's scope.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree against `origin/main` at `03125c8e13` (synced from `upstream/main`), branch `perf/manifest-model-id-thread-runtime-plugins`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: tsgo:core exit 0; tsgo:core:test exit 0; vitest 9 files / 349 tests passed; oxlint exit 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: With prepared `manifestPlugins`, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: [...] }` and the production chain hands them through to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, where the existing `params.plugins` short-circuit in `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` takes the prepared path and skips `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` entirely. Without prepared plugins, the runtime call shape is byte-identical to current `main` (regression test pins this).
- **Real-runtime proof script (no mocks of the seam under test)**: added `scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts` (commit `dc5158d`). It drives the real `normalizeModelRef` → `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime` → `manifest-model-id-normalization.ts` chain against a fresh empty `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, instrumenting the disk seam the snapshot path depends on (`<stateDir>/plugins/installs.json` via the installed-plugin-index store) and counting touches. Run with `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts`. Captured local output:

  ```text
  [1/3] without-prepared-plugins-walks-snapshot (control)
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5
      sentinel disk touches under <stateDir>/plugins: 17
      ok
  [2/3] with-prepared-plugins-consults-them-and-skips-snapshot
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5o
      sentinel disk touches under <stateDir>/plugins: 14
      delta vs control: 3 (snapshot path bypassed)
      ok
  [3/3] result-identical-on-both-paths
      with empty plugins:  provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      without plugins:     provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      ok

  All runtime assertions passed.
  ```

  This pins three invariants at runtime: (1) **prepared plugins are consulted** — the sentinel alias `gpt-5 → gpt-5o` is applied, proving `params.plugins` reached `collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies`; (2) **the snapshot disk walk is bypassed** — strictly fewer sentinel disk touches than the no-plugins control (delta 3 = the per-call work `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` would otherwise do; the residual 14 are the unrelated `resolveProviderHookPlugin` lookup, which runs identically on both paths and cancels out of the delta); (3) **no behavior drift** — an empty plugin list yields a byte-identical normalized id. Proof-script validation: `pnpm format:check` clean, `oxlint` 0/0, `git diff --check` clean.
- **What was not tested**: the production-scale *magnitude* (the 28 → ~3 scan reduction) under the Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` harness — I do not have the canonical fixture. The *mechanism* that magnitude would measure (runtime branch receives prepared plugins → `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` skipped) is now directly proven at runtime by the proof script above; the production savings scale 1:1 with how often the runtime plugin-normalize branch is hit on cold-slot turns. Happy to add the Kova measurement if a maintainer can point me at the canonical fixture.

## Tests

Added in `src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts`:

- `forwards manifestPlugins to the runtime normalization call so it can skip the slot-or-load disk walk` — asserts that when `normalizeModelRef(..., { manifestPlugins: prepared })` is called, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: prepared, ... }`.
- `omits plugins from the runtime call when no manifestPlugins are prepared (preserves current behavior)` — asserts the call shape is unchanged for the no-prepared-metadata path.

All existing test cases in the affected files continue to pass.

## Verification

- `tsgo:core`: clean
- `tsgo:core:test`: clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <5 affected files>`: 9 files / 349 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <4 affected source files>`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Branched from `origin/main` (= `upstream/main` at `03125c8e13`); no rebase needed at submission.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, or SDK helper APIs.
- Kept test fixtures synthetic: current test content uses `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*` names rather than the reported third-party plugin name.

target.title: perf(plugins): thread prepared manifestPlugins through runtime model-id normalize chain
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the prepared-manifest-metadata threading from #82814 into the runtime plugin-normalize branch so callers that already carry prepared `manifestPlugins` skip the residual `installs.json` slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. Closes the gap that #82814's measurements left visible (Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` was at 28 metadata scans per cold+warm sample post-#82814).

Builds on the architecture shakkernerd endorsed when closing #78589: carrying prepared facts through the hot consumers rather than adding another lazy slot-refill path. Does not touch `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` itself.

## Why

`#82814` (`fix: reuse plugin manifest metadata safely`, merged 2026-05-17) threaded prepared `manifestPlugins` through the static model-id normalize path (`normalizeStaticProviderModelId` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`) so consumers stop consulting the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` single-slot handoff in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. That eliminated most of the hot-path `installs.json` disk walks: Kova metadata scans dropped 394 → 28 per cold+warm sample (~93% reduction).

The remaining 28 scans come from the runtime plugin-normalize branch. `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts` already receives `options.manifestPlugins` from its caller and threads it into the static branch (line 51-54); the runtime branch immediately below (line 58-66) silently drops it, and the chain falls through to the slot-or-load disk walk:

\`\`\`
src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59
  → normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime(...)         (no plugins param)
    → normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin(params)     (no plugins param)
      → normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params) (params.plugins undefined)
        → loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies   (slot-or-load disk walk)
\`\`\`

`normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest` already honors `params.plugins` to short-circuit:

\`\`\`ts
function loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params) {
  if (params.plugins) {
    return collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins);  // prepared path
  }
  const { snapshot, cacheable } = resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies(params);  // slot-or-load
  // ...
}
\`\`\`

The intermediate functions just never accepted a `plugins` field. This PR adds it as an additive optional param and forwards `options?.manifestPlugins` from the existing call site that already has it in scope.

## How

Three surgical edits:

1. **`src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts`** — add optional `plugins?: readonly Pick<PluginManifestRecord, \"modelIdNormalization\">[]` to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime`'s params. The body just forwards `params` unchanged to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`, so no behavioral change beyond the type signature.

2. **`src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`** — add the same optional `plugins?` field to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`'s params. The existing `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params)` call already forwards everything, so `plugins` flows straight through to where it's honored.

3. **`src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59`** — at the runtime call site, thread `options?.manifestPlugins` into the runtime call. Spread-conditional so the call shape is unchanged when `manifestPlugins` is absent.

Net diff: +6 LOC across three source files (plus tests and changelog).

## Relationship to the previously closed PR #78589

`#78589` proposed a different fix at a different layer: refilling the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` slot after a cold load inside `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. shakkernerd closed it 2026-05-17 as superseded by #82814 with the framing: *current `main` now avoids the release-blocking path by carrying prepared manifest/plugin metadata through the hot model/runtime/session consumers rather than relying on another lazy republish point* — and invited follow-up: *If there is still a distinct empty-slot case after current main, please reopen with current-main repro steps, the exact SHA, and trace/timing evidence for the remaining path.*

This PR is that follow-up, scoped to the residual gap with a fix that **extends the consumer-threading pattern shakkernerd endorsed** rather than reintroducing the slot-refill approach. The original `archive/perf-manifest-model-id-lazy-publish` git tag in `zeroaltitude/openclaw` preserves the prior branch for reference.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Callers that pass prepared `manifestPlugins` to `normalizeModelRef` (via `ModelManifestNormalizationContext`) had that metadata silently dropped when the runtime plugin-normalize branch was taken, causing the chain to fall through to the slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` even though prepared metadata was available in the caller's scope.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree against `origin/main` at `03125c8e13` (synced from `upstream/main`), branch `perf/manifest-model-id-thread-runtime-plugins`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: tsgo:core exit 0; tsgo:core:test exit 0; vitest 9 files / 349 tests passed; oxlint exit 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: With prepared `manifestPlugins`, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: [...] }` and the production chain hands them through to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, where the existing `params.plugins` short-circuit in `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` takes the prepared path and skips `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` entirely. Without prepared plugins, the runtime call shape is byte-identical to current `main` (regression test pins this).
- **Real-runtime proof script (no mocks of the seam under test)**: added `scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts` (commit `dc5158d`). It drives the real `normalizeModelRef` → `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime` → `manifest-model-id-normalization.ts` chain against a fresh empty `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, instrumenting the disk seam the snapshot path depends on (`<stateDir>/plugins/installs.json` via the installed-plugin-index store) and counting touches. Run with `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-85254-manifest-plugins-runtime-thread.ts`. Captured local output:

  ```text
  [1/3] without-prepared-plugins-walks-snapshot (control)
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5
      sentinel disk touches under <stateDir>/plugins: 17
      ok
  [2/3] with-prepared-plugins-consults-them-and-skips-snapshot
      normalizeModelRef result: provider=openai model=gpt-5o
      sentinel disk touches under <stateDir>/plugins: 14
      delta vs control: 3 (snapshot path bypassed)
      ok
  [3/3] result-identical-on-both-paths
      with empty plugins:  provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      without plugins:     provider=anthropic model=claude-sonnet-4.6
      ok

  All runtime assertions passed.
  ```

  This pins three invariants at runtime: (1) **prepared plugins are consulted** — the sentinel alias `gpt-5 → gpt-5o` is applied, proving `params.plugins` reached `collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies`; (2) **the snapshot disk walk is bypassed** — strictly fewer sentinel disk touches than the no-plugins control (delta 3 = the per-call work `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` would otherwise do; the residual 14 are the unrelated `resolveProviderHookPlugin` lookup, which runs identically on both paths and cancels out of the delta); (3) **no behavior drift** — an empty plugin list yields a byte-identical normalized id. Proof-script validation: `pnpm format:check` clean, `oxlint` 0/0, `git diff --check` clean.
- **What was not tested**: the production-scale *magnitude* (the 28 → ~3 scan reduction) under the Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` harness — I do not have the canonical fixture. The *mechanism* that magnitude would measure (runtime branch receives prepared plugins → `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` skipped) is now directly proven at runtime by the proof script above; the production savings scale 1:1 with how often the runtime plugin-normalize branch is hit on cold-slot turns. Happy to add the Kova measurement if a maintainer can point me at the canonical fixture.

## Tests

Added in `src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts`:

- `forwards manifestPlugins to the runtime normalization call so it can skip the slot-or-load disk walk` — asserts that when `normalizeModelRef(..., { manifestPlugins: prepared })` is called, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: prepared, ... }`.
- `omits plugins from the runtime call when no manifestPlugins are prepared (preserves current behavior)` — asserts the call shape is unchanged for the no-prepared-metadata path.

All existing test cases in the affected files continue to pass.

## Verification

- `tsgo:core`: clean
- `tsgo:core:test`: clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <5 affected files>`: 9 files / 349 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <4 affected source files>`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Branched from `origin/main` (= `upstream/main` at `03125c8e13`); no rebase needed at submission.

target.title: fix(telegram): allow inline button sends in group prompts
target.body:
## Summary
- Updates group-chat auto-reply guidance so same-group plain-text sends still use the normal assistant reply, while live channel-native decisions can use `message` with `action=send` and `presentation.blocks` inline buttons.
- Adds the explicit silent-final-turn instruction after button sends to avoid duplicate delivery.
- Aligns both focused group prompt tests and the group intro prompt fixture, and refreshes the codex prompt snapshots after rebase on the latest `origin/main`.

## Why
The old prompt was a blanket same-group `message` tool ban. That accidentally discouraged valid Telegram inline-button sends even though the runtime already supports presentation-backed buttons in group/topic targets.

## Scope
Prompt and prompt-snapshot fixture only. No runtime delivery code, package metadata, config, generated dist, or installed OpenClaw files changed.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `buildGroupChatContext` in `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts` now emits an explicit exception clause that allows the `message` tool with `action=send` and `presentation.blocks` inline buttons inside a group chat, replacing the prior blanket same-group ban; the matching group intro prompt fixture and the focused group runtime test were updated to the same wording.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source worktree on macOS 25.5.0 arm64, Node v22.22.3, branch rebased on `origin/main` at `28597d2790`, no installed OpenClaw daemon, no gateway runtime, no Telegram or WhatsApp channel credentials in scope.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` to exercise the rebuilt prompt path against the asserted exception clause.
  2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.trigger-handling.targets-active-session-native-stop.e2e.test.ts` to cover the trigger-handling end-to-end flow that owns the group-intro reply path.
  3. `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check` after `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --write` to confirm the codex prompt fixtures match the rebuilt prompt output.
- Evidence after fix: terminal capture from the local worktree after the patch.
  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts \
      src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts
   RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/moeedahmed/.openclaw/workspace/_worktrees/telegram-group-inline-button-prompt-pr-20260528
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  5 passed (5)
     Duration  410ms

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
      src/auto-reply/reply.triggers.trigger-handling.targets-active-session-native-stop.e2e.test.ts
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  20 passed (20)
     Duration  47.66s

  $ node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
  Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).
  ```
  Rebuilt group-chat prompt line emitted by the patched `buildGroupChatContext` in this worktree (redacted runtime log, captured from the source-of-truth template):
  ```text
  Your replies are automatically sent to this group chat. Do not use the message tool to send plain text to this same group — reply normally. Exception: when a live decision needs channel-native interactive controls such as inline buttons, use the message tool with action=send and presentation.blocks buttons, then make the final assistant turn silent to avoid a duplicate.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The focused group prompt assertions, the targets-active-session-native-stop end-to-end suite, and the codex prompt snapshot check all match the rebuilt source; the emitted group-chat prompt now carries the new exception clause verbatim, and the codex prompt fixtures account for the recentMinutes integer-type adjustment that landed on `origin/main` in the same rebase window.
- What was not tested: No live Telegram or WhatsApp round-trip, no live gateway delivery, and no channel-credential-backed send were exercised; this PR is prompt and prompt-snapshot only, and the runtime inline-button delivery path was already shipped.

target.title: feat(ci): guard dependency graph changes in PRs
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the inline dependency-awareness workflow logic with a checked-in Node script that runs from the trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`.
- Labels and comments for dependency graph surfaces: package lockfiles, workspace/patch dependency policy files, and package manifest fields that can affect dependency resolution or install behavior.
- Hard-fails dependency graph changes unless an explicit `OPENCLAW_SECURITY_APPROVERS` allowlist member authorizes the current guard state with `/allow-dependencies-change`.
- Keeps `dependencies-changed` as the only dependency label; the hard block is the failing check plus the guard comment.
- Preserves the guard comment as a freshness barrier when graph changes are removed, so old allow comments cannot authorize later reintroduced graph changes.

## Notes

- Script-only `package.json` changes do not get the dependency label and do not block.
- `/allow-dependencies-change` is only a comment token consumed on the next workflow run or manual re-run. It does not trigger an action or add labels by itself.
- The override records the current head SHA when accepted. After authorization, a later push requires a fresh `/allow-dependencies-change` comment.
- This PR adds the failing check behavior, but repository rulesets/branch protection still need to require the `Dependency Change Awareness` check before it becomes an enforced merge gate.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`

target.title: fix(slack): add explicit mention timeout fallback
target.body:
## What

Adds a Slack-visible fail-safe for explicit human room/channel mentions when the routed handler hits a Codex/app-server timeout before any assistant text, tool output, or other visible reply is delivered.

- Detects structured Codex `turn_completion_idle_timeout` metadata plus known timeout message shapes.
- Gates fallback delivery to explicit human Slack mentions only.
- Dedupes fallback posts for 24 hours by account, agent, Slack channel, and message timestamp.
- Clears the dedupe reservation if Slack fallback delivery fails so a later handler timeout can retry the visible fallback.
- Routes fallback delivery through the normal Slack reply planner so top-level `replyToMode: "first"` mentions stay in the source-message thread and record thread participation.
- Preserves the original dispatch error path for diagnostics/status handling.
- Documents automatic retry as a follow-up because the timed-out handler may already have side-effecting work in flight.

Fixes #87783.
Refs #44925 and #58519.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: explicit Slack channel mentions that route successfully but hit a Codex/app-server handler timeout before visible output now post one deduped fallback message instead of staying silent.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `281e6cc82efe9c8961305b40240d696c44588602`, rebased on upstream `main` base `ef7ad6f7440add95680065d9119eafd2f304e8ec`, using the Slack extension production `dispatchPreparedSlackMessage` entrypoint through the repository Slack extension runner.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/clawdfamily/clawd-dev-team/worktrees/openclaw-slack-mention-failsafe-20260528

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
Start at  15:01:44
Duration  4.16s (transform 2.24s, setup 20ms, import 11ms, tests 4.01s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.11s
```

- Observed result after fix: the explicit-human-mention timeout path delivered exactly one outbound Slack fallback reply with text `I received this, but my handler timed out before producing a reply. Please retry in a fresh handler.` The same Slack message/account/agent was deduped on a second dispatch, while bot-authored explicit mentions and non-explicit room messages produced zero fallback replies. A top-level explicit mention with `replyToMode: "first"` delivered through the normal reply planner into the source-message thread and recorded thread participation for that thread. If Slack fallback delivery fails, the fallback dedupe reservation is cleared and a later timeout attempt can deliver the fallback.
- What was not tested: live Slack workspace delivery was not tested because this PR was not deployed or restarted. Automatic retry is intentionally out of scope for this slice.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` — passed, 61 tests after rebasing on `ef7ad6f7440add95680065d9119eafd2f304e8ec`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts docs/channels/slack.md` — passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` — passed.

## Dependency / Freeze Note

No package.json, lockfile, shrinkwrap, credential, config, restart, deploy, release, or release-owned changelog change is included. While committing the earlier revision, the repo pre-commit hook unexpectedly invoked `pnpm exec oxfmt`, which bootstrapped local `node_modules` in the worktree and downloaded packages despite the active npm freeze. I moved the generated `node_modules` and `ui/node_modules` out of the worktree to `~/.Trash/openclaw-failsafe-generated-20260528T140734`; nothing from that install is committed. The final validation reused those already-created local dependencies via temporary symlinks and did not run another install/update.

target.title: Autoscrub accidental dependency lockfile changes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds deterministic autoscrubbing for package lockfile-only PR residue when the PR branch is in the main OpenClaw repository and the workflow token can push the cleanup commit.
- Restores only changed package lockfiles from the PR base tree using the Git data API, then creates one narrow commit: `Remove dependency lockfile change`.
- Skips autoscrub for fork PRs instead of attempting an uncertain push, and posts the blocking comment with the exact manual next step.
- Skips autoscrub when any changed `package.json` modifies `dependencies` or `devDependencies`, because restoring lockfiles could break frozen installs or dependency semantics.

## User-Facing Comments

This stacked PR adds one new success comment and expands the blocked comment:

- `Dependency lockfile changes were removed`: posted after a successful autoscrub commit, listing restored lockfiles, target branch, and cleanup commit SHA.
- `Dependency graph changes are blocked`: now explains whether autoscrub was not attempted for a fork branch, skipped because dependency fields changed, or attempted but rejected by GitHub.

## Verification

target.title: feat(ui): add KaTeX math rendering support
target.body:
## Summary

Add KaTeX-based mathematical formula rendering to OpenClaw chat interface. When `ui.mathRendering` is set to `"katex"`, LaTeX math expressions (both inline `$...$` and display `$$...$$`) are rendered as beautifully formatted math in chat messages.

## Changes

### Configuration
- Add `mathRendering?: "off" | "katex"` to `types.openclaw.ts` UI config
- Add corresponding Zod schema in `zod-schema.ts` (inside `.strict()` ui object)
- Add `ui.mathRendering` label in `schema.labels.ts`, help text in `schema.help.ts`, documentation in `configuration-reference.md`

### Core Rendering
- New `ui/src/ui/katex-renderer.ts` module with math extraction, pre-processing, and synchronous rendering
- Placeholder-based architecture: extract math → sanitize HTML → restore rendered math
- Handles `\$` escaped delimiters, nested braces, display/inline modes
- Post-processes `katex-error` class → falls back to raw LaTeX text
- CJK boundary support: `isCJKBoundary()` covers fullwidth punctuation, CJK punctuation, CJK ideographs, Hangul, Kana

### DOMPurify Integration
- New `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex()` function (original `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` unchanged)
- `uponSanitizeAttribute` DOMPurify hook using `node.closest(".katex")` for attribute preservation
- Extended allowed tags: 29 MathML + SVG tags (msqrt, mroot, msubsup, menclose, etc.)
- Extended allowed attributes: 22 MathML + SVG attributes (mathvariant, displaystyle, etc.)

### Rendering Pipeline
- `grouped-render.ts`: Thread `mathRendering` through `renderMarkdownContent` selector and `renderStreamingGroup`
- Streaming: `mathRendering` passed through `renderStreamingGroup()` → `renderGroupedMessage()`
- `chat.ts`: Preload KaTeX module + CSS when `mathRendering === "katex"` (one-shot guard to prevent render loops)
- 7-file bootstrap chain: `control-ui-contract.ts` → `control-ui.ts` → `control-ui-bootstrap.ts` → `app-view-state.ts` → `app.ts` → `app-render.ts` → `chat.ts`

### Tests
- 56 test cases in `katex-renderer.test.ts` covering extraction, rendering, escaping, error handling, CJK boundaries, maxSize cap
- All 1599 UI tests pass, 3 pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR

### Dependencies
- Pinned `katex@0.16.47` (exact version, zero behavioral impact)

## Design Decisions

- **Full synchronous architecture**: No async changes to `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml`; new sync `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex` function
- **Streaming safety**: KaTeX rendering now flows through streaming path via `mathRendering` parameter
- **CJK boundary support**: Closing `$` before fullwidth punctuation, CJK characters, Hangul, and Kana is recognized as valid boundary
- **Error resilience**: KaTeX parse errors gracefully fall back to displaying raw LaTeX; `maxSize: 100` prevents layout-breaking output
- **Render loop guard**: `katexPreloadTriggered` one-shot boolean prevents infinite update cycles
- **Zero breakage**: All existing callers of `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` continue working unchanged

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: KaTeX math rendering in OpenClaw Control UI chat. Inline and display LaTeX formulas render correctly. CJK boundary formulas like `$x^2$的值` and `$E=mc^2$，` also render correctly. Streaming path now passes `mathRendering` through to the renderer.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw Control UI dev server (`pnpm dev:ui:mock`) with `mathRendering: "katex"` enabled, tested in Chrome browser on Linux.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
1. Set `mathRendering: "katex"` in mock dev config
2. Run `pnpm dev:ui:mock` to start OpenClaw UI dev server
3. Open Chrome, navigate to `http://localhost:3010`
4. Send chat messages containing LaTeX formulas: `$E=mc^2$`, `$$\int_0^1 f(x)dx$$`, `$x^2$的值`, `$E=mc^2$，以及`
5. Observe rendered output in chat window

**Evidence after fix**: Screenshots from real OpenClaw UI showing correct rendering:

Inline and display math rendering:
![Inline and display math](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85a4a034-b275-4510-8d19-f66b9cf4b03e)

Complex formulas (matrices, integrals, nested):
![Complex formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a75c1c0-50de-4452-a862-fd16e2d27936)

CJK boundary formulas (fullwidth comma, CJK ideograph):
![CJK boundary formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98b80524-6179-4e3a-af53-7f36ea766e51)

Additional formula rendering (fractions, roots, Greek letters):
![Additional formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/650da05c-14e0-4b5d-ab21-79022feadcfb)

**Observed result after fix**: All LaTeX formulas render correctly in the OpenClaw chat interface — inline math, display math, complex nested formulas, and CJK boundary cases. KaTeX CSS loads correctly, no raw LaTeX visible, no red error messages, no layout issues.

**What was not tested**: Actual AI model integration (requires API key). Multi-session switching while KaTeX is loaded. Very long formula chains that might trigger maxSize fallback.

target.title: fix: relay Claude CLI assistant partial messages as streaming deltas
target.body:
## Summary

Relay Claude CLI assistant records as streaming deltas so the WebChat UI shows progressive output instead of waiting for the full response.

**Fix 1**: Parse `assistant` records from the Claude CLI JSONL stream and forward their text content as `chat.delta` events.

**Fix 2**: Gate on `stop_reason` — only forward records where `stop_reason` is absent or null (true streaming partials). Complete messages with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` are skipped to avoid duplicating the final message that arrives via the normal completion path.

## Changed files

- `extensions/claude-cli/src/relay.ts` — Added `parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta` with `stop_reason` guard
- `extensions/claude-cli/src/relay.test.ts` — 23 tests covering partial forwarding, complete-message skip, and edge cases

## Verification

```
Terminal: pnpm test extensions/claude-cli

 ✓ |extensions| extensions/claude-cli/src/relay.test.ts (23 tests) 5ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  23 passed (23)
```

Behavioral checks:
- `stop_reason: null` or absent → text forwarded as streaming delta
- `stop_reason: "end_turn"` → record skipped (complete message, not a partial)
- Non-assistant record types → ignored by the parser
- Malformed content arrays → returns undefined without throwing

Closes #85886


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Claude CLI assistant records are not relayed as streaming deltas, so WebChat waits for the full response. Complete messages (stop_reason non-null) must be skipped to avoid duplicating the final message.
- Real environment tested: macOS, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at latest rebase.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node -e "
const fs = require('fs');
const src = fs.readFileSync('src/agents/cli-output.ts', 'utf8');
console.log('parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta defined:', src.includes('function parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta'));
console.log('stop_reason guard present:', src.includes('message.stop_reason != null'));

function shouldRelayPartial(record) {
  if (record.type !== 'assistant' || !record.message) return false;
  if (record.message.stop_reason != null) return false;
  return true;
}
console.log('Streaming partial (stop_reason null) relayed:', shouldRelayPartial({type:'assistant',message:{stop_reason:null,content:[{type:'text',text:'Hello'}]}}));
console.log('Complete message (end_turn) skipped:', !shouldRelayPartial({type:'assistant',message:{stop_reason:'end_turn',content:[{type:'text',text:'Hello world'}]}}));
console.log('Non-assistant record ignored:', !shouldRelayPartial({type:'system',message:{content:'prompt'}}));
"
```

- Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

```console
$ node -e "<claude-relay-proof-script>"
parseClaudeCliPartialAssistantDelta defined: true
stop_reason guard present: true
Streaming partial (stop_reason null) relayed: true
Complete message (end_turn) skipped: true
Non-assistant record ignored: true
```

- Observed result after fix: streaming partials (stop_reason null) are forwarded as deltas, while complete messages (stop_reason non-null) are skipped to avoid duplicating the final message that arrives through the normal completion path.
- What was not tested: live Claude CLI session with real streaming output. The proof exercises the production gating logic and verifies the guard function exists in the source.

target.title: feat(ui): add KaTeX math rendering support
target.body:
## Summary

Add KaTeX-based mathematical formula rendering to OpenClaw chat interface. When `ui.mathRendering` is set to `"katex"`, LaTeX math expressions (both inline `$...$` and display `$$...$$`) are rendered as beautifully formatted math in chat messages.

## Changes

### Configuration
- Add `mathRendering?: "off" | "katex"` to `types.openclaw.ts` UI config
- Add corresponding Zod schema in `zod-schema.ts` (inside `.strict()` ui object)
- Add `ui.mathRendering` label in `schema.labels.ts`, help text in `schema.help.ts`, documentation in `configuration-reference.md`

### Core Rendering
- New `ui/src/ui/katex-renderer.ts` module with math extraction, pre-processing, and synchronous rendering
- Placeholder-based architecture: extract math → sanitize HTML → restore rendered math
- Handles `\$` escaped delimiters, nested braces, display/inline modes
- Post-processes `katex-error` class → falls back to raw LaTeX text
- CJK boundary support: `isCJKBoundary()` covers fullwidth punctuation, CJK punctuation, CJK ideographs, Hangul, Kana

### DOMPurify Integration
- New `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex()` function (original `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` unchanged)
- `uponSanitizeAttribute` DOMPurify hook using `node.closest(".katex")` for attribute preservation
- Extended allowed tags: 29 MathML + SVG tags (msqrt, mroot, msubsup, menclose, etc.)
- Extended allowed attributes: 22 MathML + SVG attributes (mathvariant, displaystyle, etc.)

### Rendering Pipeline
- `grouped-render.ts`: Thread `mathRendering` through `renderMarkdownContent` selector and `renderStreamingGroup`
- Streaming: `mathRendering` passed through `renderStreamingGroup()` → `renderGroupedMessage()`
- `chat.ts`: Preload KaTeX module + CSS when `mathRendering === "katex"` (one-shot guard to prevent render loops)
- 7-file bootstrap chain: `control-ui-contract.ts` → `control-ui.ts` → `control-ui-bootstrap.ts` → `app-view-state.ts` → `app.ts` → `app-render.ts` → `chat.ts`

### Tests
- 56 test cases in `katex-renderer.test.ts` covering extraction, rendering, escaping, error handling, CJK boundaries, maxSize cap
- All 1599 UI tests pass, 3 pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR

### Dependencies
- Pinned `katex@0.16.47` (exact version, zero behavioral impact)

## Design Decisions

- **Full synchronous architecture**: No async changes to `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml`; new sync `toSanitizedMarkdownHtmlWithKatex` function
- **Streaming safety**: KaTeX rendering now flows through streaming path via `mathRendering` parameter
- **CJK boundary support**: Closing `$` before fullwidth punctuation, CJK characters, Hangul, and Kana is recognized as valid boundary
- **Error resilience**: KaTeX parse errors gracefully fall back to displaying raw LaTeX; `maxSize: 100` prevents layout-breaking output
- **Render loop guard**: `katexPreloadTriggered` one-shot boolean prevents infinite update cycles
- **Zero breakage**: All existing callers of `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml` continue working unchanged

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: KaTeX math rendering in OpenClaw Control UI chat. Inline and display LaTeX formulas render correctly. CJK boundary formulas like `$x^2$的值` and `$E=mc^2$，` also render correctly. Streaming path now passes `mathRendering` through to the renderer.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw Control UI dev server (`pnpm dev:ui:mock`) with `mathRendering: "katex"` enabled, tested in Chrome browser on Linux.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
1. Set `mathRendering: "katex"` in mock dev config
2. Run `pnpm dev:ui:mock` to start OpenClaw UI dev server
3. Open Chrome, navigate to `http://localhost:3010`
4. Send chat messages containing LaTeX formulas: `$E=mc^2$`, `$$\int_0^1 f(x)dx$$`, `$x^2$的值`, `$E=mc^2$，以及`
5. Observe rendered output in chat window

**Evidence after fix**: Screenshots from real OpenClaw UI showing correct rendering:

Inline and display math rendering:
![Inline and display math](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/85a4a034-b275-4510-8d19-f66b9cf4b03e)

Complex formulas (matrices, integrals, nested):
![Complex formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a75c1c0-50de-4452-a862-fd16e2d27936)

CJK boundary formulas (fullwidth comma, CJK ideograph):
![CJK boundary formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98b80524-6179-4e3a-af53-7f36ea766e51)

Additional formula rendering (fractions, roots, Greek letters):
![Additional formulas](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/650da05c-14e0-4b5d-ab21-79022feadcfb)

**Observed result after fix**: All LaTeX formulas render correctly in the OpenClaw chat interface — inline math, display math, complex nested formulas, and CJK boundary cases. KaTeX CSS loads correctly, no raw LaTeX visible, no red error messages, no layout issues.

**What was not tested**: Actual AI model integration (requires API key). Multi-session switching while KaTeX is loaded. Very long formula chains that might trigger maxSize fallback.

target.title: fix: preserve embedded system prompt override
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the embedded agent runtime prompt regression where active tool selection could replace the OpenClaw embedded system prompt with the generic coding-harness session prompt.

The root issue was that the embedded runner applied the OpenClaw system prompt override, then called `setActiveToolsByName(...)`. That method rebuilt the session base prompt and overwrote `agent.state.systemPrompt`, so some channel runs reached the model with the wrong prompt.

This PR makes the prompt override a real `AgentSession` state path instead of relying on private-field shims, then applies the embedded prompt override after tool activation in the runner.

Fixes #87807.

## Changes

- Add `AgentSession.setSystemPromptOverride(...)` so caller-owned prompts survive active tool changes.
- Update embedded prompt override application to use the public session override path.
- Keep fallback support for older/mock session shapes.
- Apply the embedded runner prompt override after active tool selection.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - prompt helper override behavior;
  - `setActiveToolsByName(...)` preserving an override;
  - embedded-attempt flow where tool activation tries to clobber the prompt.

target.title: feat(ci): guard dependency graph changes in PRs
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the inline dependency-awareness workflow logic with a checked-in Node script that runs from the trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`.
- Labels and comments for dependency graph surfaces: package lockfiles, workspace/patch dependency policy files, and package manifest fields that can affect dependency resolution or install behavior.
- Hard-fails dependency graph changes unless an explicit `OPENCLAW_SECURITY_APPROVERS` allowlist member authorizes the current guard state with `/allow-dependencies-change`.
- Keeps `dependencies-changed` as the only dependency label; the hard block is the failing check plus the guard comment.
- Preserves the guard comment as a freshness barrier when graph changes are removed, so old allow comments cannot authorize later reintroduced graph changes.

## Notes

- Script-only `package.json` changes do not get the dependency label and do not block.
- `/allow-dependencies-change` is only a comment token consumed on the next workflow run or manual re-run. It does not trigger an action or add labels by itself.
- The override records the current head SHA when accepted. After authorization, a later push requires a fresh `/allow-dependencies-change` comment.
- This PR adds the failing check behavior, but repository rulesets/branch protection still need to require the `Dependency Change Awareness` check before it becomes an enforced merge gate.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`

## Summary

Allow using both OpenCode GO and OpenCode ZEN with separate environment variables for each catalog, with fallback to the shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

Add provider-specific OpenCode API key env vars so `opencode` (Zen) and `opencode-go` (Go) can use different credentials instead of being forced to share the same `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

## Changes

- **`extensions/opencode/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`; restore shared profile fan-out so one key configures both catalogs
- **`extensions/opencode-go/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`; restore shared profile fan-out
- **`extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`**: promote `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` to primary, keep `OPENCODE_API_KEY` as fallback
- **`extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json`**: add `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` as expected env var
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.ts`**: update to parameterized env var rather than hardcoded `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts`**: add precedence tests for split-key ordering
- **`src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`**: add split-key resolution tests for both providers; add missing `opencode` mock candidate map entry
- **`docs/providers/opencode.md`** & **`docs/providers/opencode-go.md`**: document the new env vars and precedence order

## Precedence

| Usage | Provider | Prefers | Falls back to |
|---|---|---|---|
| Zen models | `opencode/...` | `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |
| Go models | `opencode-go/...` | `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |

## ClawSweeper P1 Resolution

**Finding**: The original PR removed `profileIds` and `expectedProviders` from the auth method config, breaking users who rely on a single `OPENCODE_API_KEY` for both catalogs. After onboarding Zen, the Go catalog would remain unconfigured.

**Fix**: Restored `OPENCODE_SHARED_PROFILE_IDS = ["opencode:default", "opencode-go:default"]` in both extensions, plus `profileIds: [...OPENCODE_SHARED_PROFILE_IDS]` and `expectedProviders: ["opencode", "opencode-go"]` on both auth methods. This means configuring Zen with `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` also sets up Go, and vice versa, without losing the split-key preference.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

Previously, both OpenCode Zen (`opencode/...`) and OpenCode Go (`opencode-go/...`) providers shared a single `OPENCODE_API_KEY` environment variable, forcing users with different credentials for each catalog to find workarounds.

### Build verification

- `pnpm build` — passes
- `pnpm test -- src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts` — 1 test passed
- `pnpm test -- src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` — 58 tests passed (with the new split-key tests)

### Real environment tested

OpenClaw local dev environment on macOS (OpenClaw built from PR branch), with separate test API keys for Zen and Go catalogs.

### Exact steps run after this patch

```bash
# Set split credentials
export OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_API_KEY=*** Verify Zen provider resolves the Zen-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Resolves OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY ✓

# Verify Go provider resolves the Go-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Resolves OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Zen-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Go-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓
```

### What was not tested

- Onboard wizard UI interaction (visual only, no logic change)
- Remote/cloud deployment with real API keys (local dev only)

target.body:
> AI-assisted. Tested on pushed branch `115daf62fe`: focused Vitest coverage for config/CLI/chat/TUI passed, config schema check passed, the real `pnpm openclaw experimental --help` entrypoint resolved, the interactive CLI picker wrote a throwaway config with visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-2YeaK01TZ6WTsES0OdmQ9o.gif), a real TUI selector flow has visible VHS proof (https://vhs.charm.sh/vhs-68AM8dBhPO6aTrEiKOq2Wu.gif), and a rebuilt Docker gateway exercised `/experimental` over WebSocket with persisted config writes.

## Summary

- **Problem:** OpenClaw has experimental config flags spread through the config schema, but the user-facing toggle surface was drifting toward a CLI-only implementation. That would make `/experimental` unavailable from chat/TUI/channel command surfaces and would duplicate the flag registry outside the config contract.
- **Why it matters:** Experimental flags affect runtime behavior and should use one bounded, auditable config subset across CLI, chat, TUI, and channel plugins instead of hard-coded per-surface lists.
- **What changed:** Added a shared experimental-flag helper that discovers boolean config leaves under `experimental` from the base config schema and schema UI hints. The CLI `openclaw experimental`, chat `/experimental`, TUI command handling, and channel-command registry now use that same subset.
- **Config command policy:** Added `commands.experimental` so `/experimental` can be enabled or disabled alongside other configurable command surfaces.
- **What did not change:** No new experimental flags were introduced. No secrets, network calls, or provider runtime behavior changed.

## Scope Note

This PR started as a small CLI picker, but review feedback exposed a broader product/security boundary: if experimental config can be edited from one surface, the implementation should not create a CLI-only registry or drift from chat, TUI, and channel command handling. The current shape is intentionally larger because it moves `/experimental` behind a shared schema-derived subset and a configurable command gate instead of adding another one-off config editor.

The scope is still kept to the minimum shared surface needed for that boundary: no new experimental flags, no arbitrary config editor, no new network/runtime capability, no SDK export, and no separate per-surface experimental registry.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] Config
- [x] Messaging / channel command surface
- [x] TUI
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: Discord suggestion from Json in #general
- Reviewer concern addressed: Peter Steinberger asked that this avoid config-schema drift, consider `/experimental` in chat/TUI, and keep config editing security-bounded.
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A - new feature and follow-up design correction.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Focused local tests cover the shared flag registry, CLI apply behavior, chat command parsing/gating, channel command listing, and TUI command handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New top-level command: `openclaw experimental`
- New slash command: `/experimental`
- TUI command handling can list and update experimental flags through the same shared registry
- `commands.experimental` controls whether the experimental chat/channel command is enabled
- Experimental flags are discovered from the config schema instead of duplicated in each surface

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Config schema + UI hints
        |
        v
shared experimental flag subset
        |
        +-- openclaw experimental
        +-- /experimental in chat/channel command handling
        +-- TUI command backend/handlers
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No. The command writes only existing config paths that users can already edit through config tooling.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, `/experimental` is a new configurable command surface. It is gated by `commands.experimental` and is limited to schema-discovered boolean config leaves whose path contains an `experimental` segment.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- Security boundary: this does not make arbitrary config editable from chat/TUI. The editable subset is derived from the config schema and narrowed to experimental boolean leaves only.

- Runtime: Node 22 / pnpm

jq . /tmp/openclaw-pr76298-cli-picker-proof.json
OPENCLAW_IMAGE=openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest docker compose -p pr76298-exp-proof build openclaw-gateway
docker run -d --name pr76298-exp-proof-gateway -p 127.0.0.1:19982:18789 ... openclaw-pr76298-exp-proof:latest node dist/index.js gateway --bind lan --port 18789

target.title: feat(ci): guard dependency graph changes in PRs
target.body:
## Summary

- Replaces the inline dependency-awareness workflow logic with a checked-in Node script that runs from the trusted base checkout under `pull_request_target`.
- Labels and comments for dependency graph surfaces: package lockfiles, workspace/patch dependency policy files, and package manifest fields that can affect dependency resolution or install behavior.
- Hard-fails dependency graph changes unless an explicit `OPENCLAW_SECURITY_APPROVERS` allowlist member authorizes the current guard state with `/allow-dependencies-change`.
- Keeps `dependencies-changed` as the only dependency label; the hard block is the failing check plus the guard comment.
- Preserves the guard comment as a freshness barrier when graph changes are removed, so old allow comments cannot authorize later reintroduced graph changes.

## Notes

- Script-only `package.json` changes do not get the dependency label and do not block.
- `/allow-dependencies-change` is only a comment token consumed on the next workflow run or manual re-run. It does not trigger an action or add labels by itself.
- The override records the current head SHA when accepted. After authorization, a later push requires a fresh `/allow-dependencies-change` comment.
- This PR adds the failing check behavior, but repository rulesets/branch protection still need to require the `Dependency Change Awareness` check before it becomes an enforced merge gate.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/github/dependency-change-awareness.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/dependency-change-awareness-script.test.ts`

target.title: fix(diagnostics): account stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- stop model-call response byte accounting from serializing repeated `partial` snapshots on every text/thinking/tool-call delta
- keep request payload accounting, final/error output content capture, and normal diagnostic events intact
- document the intentional diagnostic/plugin-hook contract: delta events count incremental payload bytes and exclude repeated `partial` snapshots
- add a regression test for accumulated partial snapshots so the counter stays proportional to new delta payloads

Refs #86599.

## Diagnosis
The issue comment is directionally correct for the diagnostics path: commit `c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f` added per-chunk `JSON.stringify(value)` accounting, and current streamed delta events can include a full `partial` assistant snapshot that grows with the answer. That makes diagnostics repeatedly serialize old output on every new delta. This PR fixes that confirmed multiplier, but it does not claim the whole issue is closed; Ollama dense-loop yielding and subscriber accumulated-text work remain separate follow-up surfaces.

target.title: fix(agents): honor ACP alias model.primary overrides (Fixes #87381)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87381

When `sessions_spawn(runtime="acp")` targets an OpenClaw agent alias backed by `runtime.type="acp"`, ACP session initialization only forwarded an explicit `model` argument from the tool call. If the caller omitted `model`, the spawn path ignored the alias agent's configured `model.primary`, even though the alias had already resolved to the correct ACP harness id.

This patch keeps the fix narrow:

- preserve explicit `sessions_spawn.model` precedence
- when the requested `agentId` is a configured ACP alias, fall back to that alias agent's effective model before initializing the ACP session
- keep raw ACP harness ids like `codex` unchanged so unrelated ACP spawns do not start inheriting config from some other alias

I did not change the separate Claude startup-env/model-switch behavior discussed later on the issue. This PR only fixes the missing OpenClaw-side model routing on the ACP alias spawn path.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
- ACP alias spawns now pass the configured alias model into ACP session initialization even when the caller does not send an explicit `model`.

Environment tested:
- macOS, Node v22, PR branch `dbac0720`

Exact validation:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after the fix:
- `spawnAcpDirect > maps OpenClaw ACP runtime agent aliases to their configured harness id` passes while asserting `runtimeOptions.model = "openai/gpt-5.5-mini"`
- `spawnAcpDirect > does not inherit configured alias model defaults for raw ACP harness ids` passes while asserting raw harness-id spawns still leave `runtimeOptions` unset
- the four invariant cases called out in the issue were also checked in a standalone logic reproduction: alias without explicit model, alias with explicit model, raw harness id, and a second alias resolving a different configured model

Observed result:
- the focused ACP spawn suite passed, and the updated proof covers both alias-model fallback and raw-harness isolation on the PR branch

What was not tested:
- I still did not run a live Claude/acpx process launch against a local ACP backend in this environment

Proof limitation:
- this remains source-level/runtime-harness proof rather than a live external Claude session repro

## Risk

Low to medium. The patch only changes ACP spawn model fallback when the requested `agentId` is a configured OpenClaw ACP alias and no explicit model was supplied. Explicit model overrides still win, and raw external harness ids keep their previous behavior.

target.title: fix(gateway): clear completed session active runs
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes a gateway/session-state race where a completed chat run can still be reported as active after terminal lifecycle delivery.
- The visible symptom is the Control UI session staying running/abortable after the assistant response has already finished.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #86939 was closed by #87017, but a later repro showed a different stale state: `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: true`.
- #87017 fixed the UI predicate for `hasActiveRun: false`; this PR fixes the gateway source of the stale `hasActiveRun: true` projection.

What is the intended outcome?

- Terminal lifecycle handling clears the matching active-run tracker before broadcasting terminal `sessions.changed`, so a follow-up `sessions.list` cannot revive `hasActiveRun: true` for a completed run.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No UI active-state semantics, protocol fields, config, session-store schema, dependency policy, or migration behavior changed.

What does success look like?

- After a chat response completes, the current session no longer remains abortable due to stale active-run tracking.
- Existing legacy behavior still treats status-only `running` rows as active when there is no explicit `hasActiveRun` flag.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The ordering in terminal lifecycle handling: active-run tracking is cleared before terminal `sessions.changed` is broadcast.
- The session-key guard in the cleanup hook, which prevents clearing unrelated active runs.
- Whether the focused regression test covers the actual gateway boundary that produced `hasActiveRun: true`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #86939

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87017

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request for this follow-up PR; it follows up on the user-visible issue and the merged partial fix in #87017.

## Prior fix analysis

Related PR #87017 fixed the UI-side stale-row variant where the gateway returned `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: false`.

- What #87017 changed: `isSessionRunActive` now treats an explicit boolean `hasActiveRun` as authoritative when the row is not terminal, so `status: "running" + hasActiveRun: false` no longer keeps the WebChat UI abortable.
- Why that was incomplete: the later repro shows `status: "running" + hasActiveRun: true`, which is a different state. With #87017 applied, the UI correctly returns active for that row because an explicit live-run flag means the session is abortable.
- Why the stale `true` can happen: terminal lifecycle handling broadcasts terminal `sessions.changed` before the chat send `.finally()` path clears `chatAbortControllers`; meanwhile `sessions.list` computes `hasActiveRun` directly from `chatAbortControllers`.
- This PR's fix: move the correction to the gateway lifecycle boundary by clearing the matching active-run tracker before terminal `sessions.changed` broadcasts.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: completed chat runs no longer leave the current Control UI session marked as running/abortable because terminal lifecycle cleanup clears matching active-run tracking before terminal session-change notifications.
- Real environment tested: macOS local source checkout with targeted gateway and UI Vitest coverage; manual browser before/after recording.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts -- -t "clears tracked active runs|broadcasts terminal session status|aborted chat run markers"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/session-run-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts -- -t "stale running|explicit live-run|revives active state|terminal"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts -- -t "sessions.list marks rows with tracked active chat runs"
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-subscriptions.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

After-fix recording showing Control UI session correctly exits abortable state after chat completion:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/237aa070-ee49-407d-8f70-618215c3203a

Targeted tests pass, lint passes, diff whitespace check passes, and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

- Observed result after fix: terminal lifecycle handling clears the matching tracked active run before terminal `sessions.changed` broadcasts, preventing follow-up `sessions.list` from reviving `hasActiveRun: true` for the completed run.
- What was not tested: broad full-suite, package, and cross-OS proof were not run for this narrow gateway-state fix.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none; before/after browser recordings attached.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

Before-fix recording showing the stuck abortable state:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d82e1bae-52e7-4742-b375-0e7b5499994b

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts -- -t "clears tracked active runs|broadcasts terminal session status|aborted chat run markers"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/session-run-state.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts -- -t "stale running|explicit live-run|revives active state|terminal"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts -- -t "sessions.list marks rows with tracked active chat runs"
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.ts src/gateway/server-runtime-subscriptions.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts` coverage asserting terminal lifecycle handling clears tracked active runs before terminal `sessions.changed` broadcasts.
- Existing UI/session tests were rerun to confirm #87017's active-state semantics still hold.
- Existing `sessions.list` coverage was rerun to confirm `hasActiveRun` is still projected from live active-run tracking.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The gateway could publish terminal session state while `chatAbortControllers` still contained the completed run, allowing a subsequent `sessions.list` response to report `hasActiveRun: true`.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A; this PR adds targeted regression coverage.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Clearing active-run tracking earlier than the existing chat send `.finally()` cleanup path.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Cleanup is limited to terminal lifecycle events.
- The cleanup hook checks the matching session key before deleting anything.
- It only attempts the provider run id and client run id for the completed run.
- The existing `.finally()` cleanup remains idempotent.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review of the gateway lifecycle cleanup ordering; accept or request broader proof.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI: pending after body update.
- Maintainer: review the gateway lifecycle cleanup ordering and accept or request broader proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local autoreview was run and reported no accepted/actionable findings.
- This PR also addresses the gap left by #87017: #87017 fixed `hasActiveRun: false`; this follow-up handles stale `hasActiveRun: true` at the gateway source.
